Hey guys, so welcome to the first theme plate. This is going to be the exclusively empowered call out and you'll see right next to it, we have this little notation of MVF1. So that is minimum viable funnel number one and you're going to want to pay attention to those especially if you're going to be doing those email only launches. These are the ones that I would suggest writing to create that minimum viable funnel. So anyway, this is the theme plate you use to invite prospects onto the journey and more importantly to get them excited about it through that sense of exclusive empowerment that we've been talking about throughout this module and we also answered some questions on it. So it's a simple theme plate. It's essentially going to go like this. You can open up announcing with a link what it is you're going to be inviting them to and then you could suggest that big outcome, that big outcome of your webinar series or launch, whatever it is, what are you promising, what is going to be the outcome of that and then what is that moment of highest tension. We did that exercise in module two, but to really motivate someone to enter this journey, you've got to kind of light that fire and the moment of highest tension is what turns that scrape into a cut, the paper cut into an infected wound and so forth. So you really want to agitate it by just showing what that moment of highest tension is. Then you could have your second call call to action that once again invites them, you know, right after that moment of highest tension, right when you've agitated that pain and made it present and urgent and something they need to escape. That's a good time to drop a second invitation. Then you're going to build up some interest around the event, some curiosity before ending with that exclusively empowered close. This is where you kind of set that bar of what they need to have in place, what are the prerequisites for them getting the most of this event, this webinar, this series, this launch, whatever it is, allow them to cash in on those privilege points. So an example of this is something that you may have seen. This one is pulled straight from our own recent launch. This is a preliminary draft, so it may have looked a little different from what you've seen, but in any event, it should look familiar. So, hey, I just wanted to reach out real quick. Tomorrow we'll be kicking off our brand new profit and launch workshop. You're going to learn all about how to quickly write those crazy good high converting emails that can make or break your funnel or course launch. So here right off the bat, we're leading with that big outcome. You know, the type of emails that look harmless enough on a funnel map until you realize those howling little bastards actually need tender loving care to grow and mature into automated revenue generating badasses. The types of emails that cause you to drunk post messages on Facebook at 3 AM, hoping that one of your entrepreneurial friends will magically refer you to a unicorn, aka a talented copywriter with immediate availability and won't force you to take a reverse mortgage. The kind that after realizing you can't hire out, forces you to push back your launch date by eight weeks while you rack up 361 star points at the local Starbucks and get to know every barista by name. So this is all getting super hyper specific about what that pain is. You know, that is the moment of highest tension. And when you can reflect that back right off the bat, you're really building that trust and that presupposition that you know a better way. Not a fan of Starbucks rewards. Don't miss this workshop damage. So this is this call to action right after that moment of highest tension. This is a fun way to kind of like frame your call to actions. It doesn't always have to be show up or click here or join the workshop. You know, sometimes you could actually frame it in a way that that's included in the story you're telling. The other thing I want to talk about here is just get hyper specific with things that are familiar to your prospect. You know, whether that's Starbucks reward points, you know, that's something we could all really relate to and it just makes it feel like what you're offering matches that reality or your prospects reality. Then we have a quick sign off and a PS is this a can't miss. Honestly, I have no clue. You tell me. I mean, I wouldn't offer you an extra ticket to a radio head show unless I knew for certain that you enjoyed listless walks in the rain and waxing philosophical over existentialism and other topics that make the average person's head spin. Same goes here. We're going to pack this 60 minute show with an epic set list. Play the crowd pleasers like how copywriting has changed over the last few years and how to take full advantage of those recent shifts in your next launch. So these are just going to be points to sell the event. I'm not going to read through them all right now, but essentially what we are doing is we aren't assuming that they're perfect for it. We're actually being very upfront. I do not know if this is going to be perfect for you. This is what you're going to get. Those are the benefits. So we're selling the event and then here's where we get into the exclusive empowerment. You should only make it to the show if you should only show up if you should only join my webinar if so that's kind of the lead in to what an exclusive empowered customer will be. So what can we where can we give them a chance to cash in? I was going to say they're Starbucks points, but they're privileged points. They're exclusivity points. So if you're an online entrepreneur, coach, consultant, expert, or influencer who already has a product you're crazy proud of or will in the near future and want to be able to 2x, 3x, or 5x sales with powerful and easy to implement launch copy. This be me. I'm in. You're an established email or launch copyright who charges the big bucks to do it for the people above and wants to charge the even bigger bucks by getting better results in less time. Damn, it's like you read my mind. And then we just kind of close off the whole Starbucks joke. You won't miss racking up the Starbucks points knowing full well you'll be able to afford your own denti five shot flat whites. Thank you very much. So I don't know why I said it that way. Probably because that's how I order my own five shot flat white lattes. But anyway, so that is where you include those exclusive empowerment. Exclusive empowerment items, if you will, and really give them a chance to cash in on those points. And then advanced and optional strategy is this could be a way to segment your different buckets, you know, what we did here. You know, the first call out was for online entrepreneurs, coaches, and consultants. The second one was for copywriters, you know, and the third one was for just people who want to buy their own lattes. We did not do anything with that bucket, but maybe we could maybe we're going to strike up a partnership with Starbucks or something. But anyway, that is the first email, the first theme plate. And I really want you to explore this, make sure you have your exclusive empowerment points down because that is really the crux of this one, that plus a moment of highest tension. So give that a go and then I will meet you in the next theme plate, which is going to be borrowing a little help from a famous little Canadian pop star. So I'll see you there in a sec.
